title: Schilleria callosa
taxonomy: Schilleria callosa is classified within the family Celastraceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the bittersweet or staff vine family. Schilleria callosa belongs to the order Celastrales, which is a division of angiosperms characterized by their unique flower and fruit structures. The classification of Schilleria callosa is further supported by its physical and morphological characteristics, such as its woody stems and pinnate leaves. Within the Celastraceae family, Schilleria callosa is one of the many species found in various parts of the world.

intro: Schilleria callosa is a perennial plant species within the family Annonaceae, native to tropical Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Schilleria callosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Schilleria callosa is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to create syrups, ointments, and other topical preparations.
